title: National Security
description: RAND conducts a broad array of national security research for the U.S. Department of Defense and allied ministries of defense. Our federally funded research and development centers (FFRDCs) explore threat assessment, military acquisition, technology, recruitment and personnel management, counterinsurgency, intelligence, and readiness.
text:
How Russia Is Intercepting Communications from European Satellites
Russia has been secretly stalking European spacecraft since its invasion of Ukraine in 2014. What can Europe do about it?
Understanding China’s National Security Decisionmaking: A Primer for U.S. Policymakers
The authors provide a framework for describing the foundational elements that shape how governments make national security decisions—information, analysis, and authorities—and apply that framework to the People’s Republic of China.
Assessing the Practical Feasibility of the Clader-Jacobs-Sprouse Quantum Algorithm for Calculating Radar Cross Sections
The authors of this report examine the Clader-Jacobs-Sprouse quantum computing algorithm's potential practical utility for modeling a target's radar cross section.
I Knew the Data on Caregivers. My Stroke Made It Real
RAND researcher Rajeev Ramchand has studied caregiving for years. But it took having a stroke to drive home how hidden some aspects of caregiving remain.
Pursuing Disarmament, Demobilization, and Reintegration in Gaza: A Critical Pathway to a Durable Peace
The authors propose a disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR) framework for Gaza. They also describe the preconditions necessary for a successful DDR program in Gaza, including the link to deradicalization.
State-Level Summaries of Veteran Needs and Well-Being
The authors of this report present state-level estimates related to veterans’ health, well-being, and economic conditions. These estimates are presented to help inform planning, program development, and resource allocations for veterans.
Russia in the High North: Russian Strategy and Escalation Risks in the High North After the Russia-Ukraine War
The enlargement of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization has changed Russia’s perception of risk in the High North. RAND researchers analyzed escalation scenarios to understand Russia’s priorities and activity in the region after the war in Ukraine.
Evaluating the Impact of Changes to the Air Force Officer and Enlisted Evaluation Systems
In 2023, the U.S. Air Force implemented several changes to the officer and enlisted performance evaluation systems. In this report, the authors document findings and recommendations from their analysis and evaluation of those changes.
The Weapons of Mass Destruction AI Security Gap
The AI ecosystem may be too narrowly focused on a single threat model: the “lone wolf virus terrorist.” This emphasis on individual actors could leave state-based and terrorist group threats dangerously under-examined.
Why Korea's Nuclear-Powered Submarine Matters to U.S. Strategy
A nuclear-powered submarine would not just be a Korean capability upgrade. In an era of intensifying undersea competition and growing demands on U.S. forces, this is a strategic opportunity for the alliance, as well as for security in the Indo-Pacific.
Manpower Analysis to Improve the Functional Alignment and Organizational Structure of Space Training and Readiness Command Headquarters
The U.S. Space Force’s Space Training and Readiness Command does not have the manpower to meet its requirements. To address this, RAND researchers determined its manpower needs, created a model for staffing decisions, and assessed its structure.
